title: Accessible Combobox Autocomplete
description:
Build an **accessible combobox/autocomplete** that follows the WAI-ARIA combobox pattern with listbox popup.

### Requirements

- Input has `role="combobox"`, `aria-expanded`, `aria-autocomplete="list"`, `aria-controls` pointing to the listbox
- Listbox popup has `role="listbox"`; each option has `role="option"`
- The active option is tracked with `aria-activedescendant` on the input
- **Arrow Down/Up** navigates options in the listbox
- **Enter** selects the highlighted option
- **Escape** closes the listbox and clears the highlight
- Typing filters options (case-insensitive)
- Selected option appears in the input; listbox closes
- When no options match, show a "No results" message with `role="status"`
- The listbox appears when the input is focused and has text

### Test IDs

| Element | data-testid |
|---------|-------------|
| Combobox input | `combo-input` |
| Listbox | `listbox` |
| Option item | `option-{index}` |
| Selected display | `selected-value` |
| No results | `no-results` |
constraints:
- Must use aria-activedescendant (not roving tabindex) for option focus
- Input must keep focus at all times (even while navigating options)
- Must follow WAI-ARIA combobox 1.2 pattern
- Filtering must be case-insensitive
- Mouse click on an option must also work
hints: aria-activedescendant tells the screen reader which option is 'focused' while the real DOM focus stays on the input.
Each option needs a unique `id` attribute. Set `aria-activedescendant` on the input to the id of the active option.
On Arrow Down/Up, update the active index. Scroll the active option into view with `scrollIntoView({ block: 'nearest' })`.
